Name: Ajay Bhargava
Bio: Ajay Bhargava runs a high-volume practice for Indian conglomerates, multinationals, and government bodies, with instructions from Aditya Birla Group, Adani Group, L&T, Siemens, Hyundai, Panacea and INOX. Complexity is among his strongest RISE indicators, with matters regularly involving multiple jurisdictions, public sector counterparties, and points of law with no settled precedent. A standout recent matter is Antrix Corporation, the first case in India to establish a doctrine of fraud in arbitration, with enforcement proceedings running in parallel across the Netherlands, Germany, France, and the US. His RISE sophistication score is anchored in talent development, working directly on matters alongside junior lawyers and having mentored around 15 lawyers to partnership over his career.

Name: Ashim Sood
Bio: Ashim Sood runs a deliberately constrained practice built around depth rather than volume, concentrating on a small number of matters at a time. Direct senior involvement is the product rather than an occasional feature. His RISE instructions scores reflect this: the work he takes on is typically complex in legal rather than financial terms, gravitating toward jurisdictional questions, corporate veil arguments, and cases where the applicable framework is genuinely unsettled. His sophistication score is anchored in how he deploys technology. He uses AI primarily as a verification layer to identify contradictions across documents and cross-check witness statements against correspondence, and is building a knowledge management system to retrieve prior research systematically across matters.

Name: Ashish Bhan
Bio: Ashish Bhan has been at Trilegal since 2010 and leads a team of 25 to 30 lawyers, with arbitration accounting for around 45 to 50% of his personal practice. AI usage drives his strong RISE sophistication score. Running searches across Trilegal's 25-year database via Lucio, his team recently identified eight recurring risk areas in a defence tender in days rather than the two days of manual file review previously required. Defence and aviation manufacturers form the core of his client base, with a practice specialising in procurement arbitrations involving G2G contracts, geopolitical sensitivities, and strategic decisions about which claims to press and which to concede to preserve government relationships across jurisdictions.

Name: Binsy Susan
Bio: Binsy Susan's RISE instructions scores are her strongest metric, built on a genuinely multi-jurisdictional practice spanning SIAC, ICC, AAA, and CAM CCBC, with matters seated across Singapore, Brazil, the US, and India. Few India-based practitioners can match that breadth. Reputation scores are solid, underpinned by consistent directory recognition and a social media presence that keeps her visible beyond the immediate legal market. Her instruction base is distinctive in sector range, spanning technology, construction, oil and gas, energy, infrastructure, and telecoms alongside the more typical M&A, JV and shareholder disputes.

Name: Chakrapani Misra
Bio: Chakrapani Misra has practised arbitration for over 33 years, beginning under the 1940 Act before the current framework existed. His RISE sophistication score is his most prominent metric, built through a pricing score above the median. His instruction base reflects a predominantly domestic practice of high legal complexity. Standout recent matters include a procedurally challenging ropeway project arbitration for a major Mumbai temple and an ICC cross-border money claim for a Mauritius-based company against an Indian multinational subsidiary.

Name: Dheeraj Nair
Bio: Dheeraj Nair's RISE instructions metrics reflect high volume across complex mandates, concentrated in private equity shareholders' disputes, and technically complex arbitrations in infrastructure, oil and gas sectors. These span international, domestic, institutional, and ad hoc proceedings alongside white collar and insolvency work. A standout matter involved defending three major PE investors against an emergency SIAC injunction application, where he not only resisted the order but secured one against the counterparty. He focuses on identifying settlement windows early and delivering outcomes quickly, qualities reflected in his strong RISE experience metrics.

Name: Ila Kapoor
Bio: Ila Kapoor's RISE reputation metrics place her among the leading practitioners in this cohort, with strong instructions scores reflecting her consistent role on high-value disputes. Her practice is increasingly focused on private equity matters, where she advises foreign PE firms and Indian promoters on exit and post-acquisition claims, alongside a steady flow of hospitality arbitrations. She also handles construction disputes with international counterparties and multi-jurisdictional commercial arbitrations. She studied law in the UK, worked in the US and conducts her own advocacy rather than routinely briefing senior counsel, supporting her cross-border approach.

Name: Indranil D Deshmukh
Bio: Indranil D Deshmukh holds one of the strongest experience scores in this cohort, reflecting positive client feedback across all experience sub-indicators. His RISE instructions scores are equally solid, with a client base including Larsen & Toubro, CG Power, the Board of Control for Cricket in India, Hindustan Petroleum, Greenko Group, and Maharashtra State Road Development Corporation. His practice specialises in construction, EPC, oil and gas, infrastructure, and media rights arbitrations, frequently with government counterparties including MMRDA and ONGC. Deshmukh's RISE scores indicate a practice that completes solid, legally complex work rather than prioritising a loud external reputation.

Name: Kapil Arora
Bio: Kapil Arora's RISE instructions metrics are built on a concentrated book of repeat international clients in construction and infrastructure arbitration. Clients include Larsen & Toubro, Sembcorp Green Infra and Hellmann Worldwide Logistics, placing him at the higher end of the market for cross-border engineering disputes. Fifteen years of accumulated technical fluency allows him to explain turbine and boiler mechanics to tribunals himself. His Talent Development sub-indicator is also strong as he gives junior lawyers early exposure to high-stakes advocacy, producing a top-heavy team with negligible attrition. All advocacy and cross-examination is conducted in-house rather than briefing external counsel.

Name: Ketan Gaur
Bio: Ketan Gaur holds rights of audience before the Singapore International Commercial Court and Court of Appeal, a practical credential that reflects the genuine shape of his practice. His mandates span Singapore, London, Germany, and India, and he routinely handles arbitration alongside the simultaneous enforcement, set-aside, and interim relief proceedings that follow. His RISE instructions score is one of his strongest metrics, with a near-perfect complexity rating, built on a client base that includes sovereign-linked entities alongside multinationals. He is appointed as arbitrator by Indian constitutional courts in ad hoc proceedings, and he regularly advises general counsel on operational and strategic questions beyond the formal mandate, giving him unusual commercial depth for a partner at his stage.

Name: Kingshuk Banerjee
Bio: Kingshuk Banerjee concentrates almost exclusively on arbitration, with 90% of his billable work in the practice. His focus is M&A and shareholder disputes, cross-border private equity investment claims, and a small number of construction, maritime, and defence matters. His RISE instructions score reflects high volume and legal complexity, with a client base that includes a major mining company, Tarini Prasad Mohanty, and Maxis Communications Berhad. His RISE reputation score is built through consistent directory recognition and a strong social media presence rather than purely through peer citation. He handles advocacy in-house rather than briefing external senior counsel, giving clients continuity between strategy and courtroom presentation. What distinguishes him is a tendency toward deep over-preparation, a discipline he credits for consistently producing results rather than treating as inefficiency.

Name: Lomesh Kiran Nidumuri
Bio: Lomesh Kiran Nidumuri's RISE instructions scores are his strongest metric, with complexity as the standout indicator. He acted as sole arbitrator in a Singapore-seated, Singapore law-governed SIAC arbitration involving parties from Singapore, Bangladesh, and Japan in a loan agreement dispute, delivering the award himself. His practice handles complex commercial arbitrations across tech, fintech, start-ups, and unicorn disputes, frequently drawing on internal experts across tax, foreign exchange, and real estate. Talent development is also strong within his RISE sophistication scores. He values intellectual curiosity and resilience, runs regular internal sessions, and brings in external speakers on specialist areas including international arbitration drafting. His hands-on approach extends to conducting pleadings, cross-examination, and arguments himself across all matters.

Name: Manmeet Singh
Bio: Manmeet Singh joined Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas in December 2024 from Saraf and Partners, bringing a practice concentrated in high-value infrastructure and construction disputes for Indian conglomerates, private equity platforms, and energy companies. His RISE instructions scores reflect a preference for complex, big-ticket matters over volume, with six to seven arbitrations a year each typically involving multiple expert workstreams across delay, quantum, and technical disciplines. His RISE sophistication score is also strong, underpinned by two habits that are more systematic than most. His team uses AI daily for document review, bundle summarisation, and cross-examination preparation, and he tracks court judgements closely to monitor the evolution of arbitration and contract law in India, feeding that analysis directly into how he builds legal arguments. What sets him apart is the investment he makes in anticipatory strategy, modelling how opposing counsel will frame a set of facts and how a tribunal is likely to read them before a hearing stage is reached.

Name: Manoj K Singh
Bio: Manoj K Singh's RISE instructions metrics are among the highest in this cohort by volume, built on a client base spanning NHAI, Adani Ports, Larsen & Toubro, GMR Group, Jindal Group, and the Dedicated Freight Corridor Corporation, concentrated in infrastructure and construction arbitration across ports, airports, metro, rail, roads, and mining. AI usage is notable within his RISE sophistication scores. He uses Harvey and Relativity for e-discovery across matters involving two to five million pages of documentation, runs extensive internal training programmes across AI and discovery tools, and believes AI adoption is non-optional for practitioners who want to remain competitive. His differentiating model is end-to-end: damages analysis, technical and legal discovery, witness strategy, and advocacy are all handled internally rather than outsourced, a level of integration he sees as central to how his team delivers on complex mandates.

Name: Naresh Thacker
Bio: Naresh Thacker established his own chambers in 2025 after 17 years in arbitration, most of them at Economic Laws Practice where he built the disputes practice from a base in regulatory and tax work. Thacker runs 11 to 12 matters a year at the higher end of ticket size, weighted toward technically demanding construction, dredging, and freight corridor disputes alongside shareholder work. His client base spans both direct corporate instructions and law firms that brief him as counsel. What distinguishes him for instructing firms is a fluency in translating legal strategy into communication that reaches client management directly, something he attributes to having spent over a decade operating inside a law firm before moving to independent practice.

Name: Nitesh Jain
Bio: Nitesh Jain joined Trilegal in 2021 to strengthen the disputes practice. His RISE instructions score is his strongest metric, built on high-value cross-border mandates for clients including Blackstone, Mitsui and Zee Entertainment across seats in Singapore, London, and New York. A standout matter involved winning a SIAC arbitration, and successfully enforcing and implementing a foreign emergency order through to the Indian Court using an indirect mechanism. His RISE sophistication score reflects deliberate investment in talent. He divides cross-examination responsibilities between partners and team members, organised a mock cross-examination trial attended by around 100 general counsel and lawyers, and sends team members to conduct their own advocacy in domestic proceedings to build the instincts that complex international arbitrations require.

Name: Priyanka Shetty
Bio: Priyanka Shetty scores strongly on the RISE instructions metric, reflecting a practice where arbitration routinely generates parallel NCLT proceedings, criminal complaints, and urgent protective applications that most practices are not structured to handle simultaneously. AI usage and her approach to Talent Development are notable within her RISE sophistication metrics. She deploys Harvey systematically, runs a PQE-tiered judgment review programme, and has published on AI in arbitration. A standout matter involved enforcement of a SIAC emergency award resulting in asset freezes, passport suspension, and a custodial sentence, sitting at the outer edge of Indian enforcement proceedings. She is currently advising Pratt & Whitney in a $7.3bn SIAC arbitration testing whether an Indian insolvency moratorium can halt a foreign-seated proceeding.

Name: Rajendra Barot
Bio: Rajendra Barot at AZB & Partners holds the highest RISE instructions score in this rating and receives consistent peer citation across the market. His practice combines a decade of prior corporate M&A experience with disputes work, giving him command of transactional documents and commercial rationale that is relatively unusual in the arbitration bar. He acts for major private equity firms including Westbridge Ventures and BanyanTree Growth Capital. Recent instructions include matters exceeding $1bn in value, with enforcement regularly contested through the Indian courts. That combination of reputational standing, instruction volume, and cross-border complexity places him at the top of this cycle.

Name: Sameer Tapia
Bio: Founder and senior partner of ALMT Legal, Sameer Tapia's RISE instructions metrics are his strongest, driven by a client base of European, American, and Far Eastern multinationals alongside large Indian conglomerates, most of whom mandate him directly against Tier 1 Indian firms. His reputation score is built less through directory recognition than through an active presence in the business press and a social media profile that keeps him visible well beyond the legal market. The firm's UK roots and seats across London, Singapore, and Dubai reflect a practice that has been cross-border in character from the outset. He uses AI for case law research only, treating it as a probability tool rather than a substitute for commercial judgement. He frames junior development around the distinction between grinders, minders, and finders as a map for career progression.

Name: Sanjeev K Kapoor
Bio: Sanjeev Kapoor at Khaitan & Co is first in this RISE arbitration ranking, with a practice that is both high in volume and unusually wide in sectoral range. Matters include a $6bn bilateral investment treaty claim against India, software development disputes, large-scale energy and steel sector matters, and construction liability cases, with around 60% of his work cross-border. His RISE reputation score is among the strongest in this cohort, built on consistent directory recognition and frequent citation by peers across both domestic and cross-border proceedings, including by practitioners at international firms he regularly faces. He handles advocacy and cross-examination in-house rather than briefing senior counsel, and has structured his team around sector-specific partner specialisation so that technical depth in technology, energy, and infrastructure is backed by dedicated expertise. He currently uses AI for chronologies, document contrast, and initial research.

Name: Shaneen Parikh
Bio: Shaneen Parikh's RISE instructions metrics are her strongest, driven by a concentrated focus on international arbitration at one of India's largest firms. Her client base sits at the upper end of the market, weighted toward pre and post-M&A disputes, JV breakdowns, and shareholder issues for PE investors. Dual-qualified in India and England and Wales, registered as a foreign lawyer with the SICC, and having served two terms on the SIAC Court, few India-based practitioners can match that institutional standing. She is also a member of the Governing Board of ICAA, and a Vice-Chair of the ICC Commission on Arbitration and ADR. Parikh's RISE reputation metrics are among the strongest in the cohort, with consistent peer recommendations, directory presence, and a strong news and social media profile.

Name: Shankh Sengupta
Bio: Shankh Sengupta has built a practice spanning shareholder and exit disputes, infrastructure and construction arbitrations, insolvency, and white collar defence. Clients include Macquaire, Glenmark, 3i Group, PSP, National High Speed Rail, Doosan Power Systems, and Nayara Energy. AI usage is notable within his RISE sophistication metrics. He uses Lucio and Relativity for document management, particularly in construction arbitrations handling thousands of documents, alongside a firm knowledge management database tracking how arguments have fared in past proceedings. He runs regular check-ins with clients at interlocutory stages to identify settlement windows rather than running matters to final award.

Name: Smarika Singh
Bio: Smarika Singh's RISE instructions metrics reflect a practice concentrated in legally complex mandates, with JV disputes and infrastructure arbitrations forming a consistent and technically demanding base for both domestic and international clients. She maintains personal command of every file, leading proceedings independently. Her talent development sub-indicator is also strong, with associates given live appearances before retired Supreme Court judges at the lower-stakes end. A standout matter is a JV dispute that has expanded into three simultaneous arbitrations under three separate agreements, with the same parties and interlinked breaches requiring a dedicated team to coordinate across all proceedings at once.

Name: Sneha Jaisingh
Bio: Sneha Jaisingh's RISE instructions metrics are among the highest by volume in this cohort, built on a consistently busy docket across commercial, infrastructure, and government-facing mandates. Ticket sizes sit at the mid-market level, but instruction frequency places her above most peers on volume. Clients include Adani Enterprises, Hindustan Petroleum Corporation, Larsen & Toubro, Vedanta, and Vodafone Idea. Her talent development sub-indicator is strong. Juniors run weekly round-ups presenting the latest decisions to the full team and are given low-value court appearances to build advocacy confidence before moving to more complex mandates. She argues her own mandates rather than briefing external counsel. A standout matter is the Prysmian LCIA arbitration, where the Supreme Court delivered one of India's first detailed judgments on the scope of judicial interference with foreign arbitral awards.

Name: Sooraj Sharma
Bio: Sooraj Sharma is a disputes lawyer based in Delhi and founding partner of Clavius Legal. His practice leans towards international arbitration, with a proportion dedicated to domestic arbitration and strategic advisory work. His highest RISE metric is instructions. He accumulated a notable number of international instructions over the research period, reflecting the firm's deliberate positioning at the cross-border end of the market. He acts for Indian and foreign corporates, sovereign clients, and high-net-worth individuals in complex cross-border disputes and investigations. Reputation scores are more limited, reflecting the boutique's lower market visibility relative to full-service firms. Sharma has onboarded multiple AI tools and is actively evaluating legal-specific platforms.

Name: Suvigya Awasthy
Bio: Suvigya Awasthy's RISE instructions metrics reflect a busy mid-ticket practice at a boutique, with volume and complexity ratings that hold up against larger-firm peers. His client base is anchored by government-linked infrastructure bodies including the Dedicated Freight Corridor and the National High Speed Railway Corporation, with work spanning construction, energy, infrastructure, gaming, and paper mills. A standout matter is the successful challenge of an ICC award before the SICC on grounds of plagiarism, a globally cited decision that sits above the complexity level his ticket size would typically suggest. He conducts his own advocacy before tribunals and the Supreme Court rather than briefing external counsel, keeping costs contained in a way that mid-market clients value.

Name: Tine Abraham
Bio: Tine Abraham specialises in infrastructure and concession arbitrations at Trilegal, with a practice spanning dams, roads, airports, renewable energy projects, shareholder disputes and statutory arbitrations arising from market intermediary obligations. Her RISE instructions score is one of her strongest metrics, built on a high-quality client base including India RF, Quest Global group and WeWork India. Her sophistication score reflects a systematic approach to technology. Trilegal's custom-built AI platform is used across her practice for document classification, chronology building, evidence categorisation, and translation of vernacular materials into English, an integration that most practitioners have not yet adopted. A recent standout matter involved a concession dispute with the Government of Gujarat over ferry operations, which ran to approximately 60 days of cross-examination before reaching closing arguments.

Name: Vijayendra Pratap Singh
Bio: Vijayendra Pratap Singh's most notable RISE indicator is reputation. He is consistently cited by peers across the Indian disputes market, setting him apart in this cohort. His RISE instructions scores are also strong, reflecting sustained involvement in high-value arbitration matters. He has acted on significant mandates, including representing PS Toll Road in a high-value EPC dispute against NHAI, securing interim relief to protect toll revenues and successfully defending that position before the Delhi High Court. His client base includes major international technology companies and large domestic conglomerates. Singh also holds positions on the Court of SIAC, the Oman Arbitration Centre Executive Committee and the LCIA Asia User Council. He teaches at two universities and runs internal training programmes. He describes his approach as a win or learn philosophy.

Name: Vyapak Desai
Bio: Vyapak Desai established his own chambers in August 2025 after nearly 20 years at Nishith Desai Associates, where he built the arbitration practice from scratch into one of the firm's most significant verticals. His RISE reputation score is among the strongest in this cohort. He sits on the board of the Mumbai International Arbitration Centre and acts as Vice President of Arbitration of India, is consistently cited across legal directories, and carries a peer profile that few independent counsel in India can match. His instruction base reflects a practice built from the outset around non-Indian clients and non-Indian law, having handled arbitrations under English, Qatari, Finnish, Singaporean, and BIT regimes. Recent matters include a multibillion-dollar investment treaty claim by a Malaysian telecoms company against the Government of India and a Singapore-seated acquisition dispute against one of the world's largest cryptocurrency platforms. A transactional background in M&A and capital markets gives him an analytical lens that shapes how he approaches complex commercial disputes.

Name: Gunita Pahwa
Bio: Gunita Pahwa's RISE instructions scores are her strongest metric, built on a client base concentrated in national ports and state and regional governments. This produces consistently large-ticket mandates and repeat instructions that few infrastructure practitioners outside the top-tier firms can match. Reputation metrics are supported less by directory recognition than by a strong social media presence and regular news coverage, reflecting a market profile built on visibility as much as peer citation. Her practice spans ports, roads, and airports with a dedicated focus on public sector undertakings and government contracts, extending to international proceedings at SIAC, ICC Paris, and Nepal.

Name: Aditya Bhargava
Bio: Sitting in 9th position for the 2026 RISE debt capital markets ranking, Aditya Bhargava's practice scores most highly for its sophistication, with standout RISE metrics in talent development and AI usage. According to Bhargava, his practice generates 200,000 to 300,000 documents a year, and his team is building an in-house document management system with natural language search across historical knowledge to identify precedents and generate draft language. His team model is deliberately low-leverage and long-tenured, with counsels and senior associates remaining for multiple years, supported by mentoring and a culture of open debate. Moreover, clients enjoy working with him as shown by his high scores in RISE experience, notably for commerciality, network and strategy skills.

Name: Manisha Shroff
Bio: Manisha Shroff at Khaitan & Co receives more peer recommendations than any other practitioner in the DCM list and her RISE reputation scores are among the strongest in this year's rating. Her media presence and social media following reflect a market profile built over consistent years at the top end of the market. She leads one of the few purely DCM focused practices in India, a specialism that is genuinely rare given the ECM orientation of most Indian capital markets work. Shroff's instruction record is market leading on volume and value of corporate bond issuances, spanning multiple Vedanta bonds, JSW Steel, airport and infrastructure bonds, and NBFC offshore debt, a sector that has grown significantly as companies seek cheaper international funding. Axis Bank programme updates and recurring infrastructure mandates round out a practice that operates across the full range of Indian corporate debt.

Name: Niloufer Lam
Bio: With a high RISE score in instructions, Niloufer Lam's practice consists of a  high number of complex, medium-to-big ticket mandates. Lam's RISE reputation score demonstrates that she is well perceived across legal directories and in the media, but receives less recognition from her peers. Her client base centres on large public sector and infrastructure-linked issuers in power and energy, including Power Finance Corporation, REC, Power Grid, Indian Oil, and Hindustan Petroleum, alongside Tata and Axis entities. Power Finance Corporation's $8bn GMTN programme and REC's first market-linked deep-discount issuance, a first-of-its-kind structure in India, are the clearest illustrations of where her practice operates. A background spanning multiple jurisdictions and investment banking informs how she translates complex Indian regulatory issues for international clients.

Name: Priyanka Kumar
Bio: Priyanka Kumar at TT&A has high RISE instructions and reputation scores, reflecting a strong client base, volume, and complexity of work. Her client base is anchored by major global banks including JPMorgan, Citi, Barclays, Deutsche Bank, HSBC, and Standard Chartered, alongside significant Indian financial institutions. Her practice sits at the intersection of regulatory advisory and transactional execution, with close involvement throughout, an approach she says reduces the friction that delays cross-border deals. Her instruction record reflects a broader market trend of Indian companies accessing international bond markets across new sectors, with the complexity in her work driven by first-of-their-kind structures that align domestic legal requirements with international capital market standards.

Name: Rahul Gulati
Bio: Rahul Gulati at TT&A's scores most strongly on the RISE instructions metric, performing exceptionally well for his client base, volume and value of work. Gulati's scores reflect a DCM practice that operates consistently at the complex end of the market. His instruction record includes India's first dollar bond asset-backed securitisation for Sriram Finance, the first international bond issuance by an Indian road company, and Biocon's debut pharmaceutical bond transactions, each requiring new structuring techniques that aligned Indian legal frameworks with international capital markets standards. His approach combines regulatory advisory with transactional execution, with both partners closely involved throughout and regulatory issues identified early, a method he says keeps cross-border deals moving where coordination across multiple stakeholders would otherwise create delays.

Name: Satadru Goswami
Bio: Sophistication is Satadru Goswami's strongest RISE score, with talent development as the standout metric. He leads a twelve-lawyer team with a deliberately flat structure, rejecting the traditional pyramid model in favour of close partner involvement on every transaction and early responsibility for associates. His practice is structuring-led, advising on offshore and domestic bond issuances for major infrastructure and real asset platforms including ASK Group, Edelweiss Alternative Asset Advisors (EAAA), Adani group entities, and Government of Maharashtra instrumentalities, as well as private credit and structured debt products across infrastructure and non-infrastructure sectors, including investments through AIF and InvIT platforms. Goswami maintains detailed precedent databases, integrates lessons from banking and project finance into capital markets mandates, and his mandates span a range of fee arrangements depending on the nature and structure of the transaction.

Name: Shubhangi Garg
Bio: Shubhangi Garg scores most highly across RISE metrics instructions and sophistication, demonstrating advanced AI usage and attention to talent development. Her team uses AI in a targeted rather than wholesale way, applying it to refine contractual provisions, translate complex clauses for clients, and run quick diligence checks, with the main benefit being time compression. Her practice is split across REITs and InvITs, corporate and performing credit, acquisition finance, and international dollar bond mandates in financial services and renewables. Two characteristics she is deliberate about are: a solution-driven negotiation style, and a team culture where stress comes from client work rather than internal dynamics.

Name: Uttara M. Kolhatkar
Bio: Uttara M Kolhatkar at JSA Advocates & Solicitors, receives the second highest RISE reputation score, with peer recommendations and media profile both performing well within the RISE metrics. In instructions, Kolhatkar's work value and client roster of major international banks, leading issuers and other arrangers stand out. Her practice focuses majorly on offshore bonds, covering Rule 144A and Regulation S standalone issuances across vanilla EMTN and GMTN programmes, green bonds, sustainability-linked bonds, Samurai bonds, Formosa bonds, and orphan SPV structures, one of the more technically demanding spheres of Indian debt capital markets. JSA is among very few top-tier Indian firms active in this space, and Kolhatkar runs that capability alongside a single senior partner, which gives the practice an unusual concentration of expertise at the top.

Name: Subhalakshmi Naskar
Bio: Subhalakshmi Naskar at Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas is rated across three RISE lists: infrastructure, private capital, and debt capital markets. Across these categories, Naskar's highest RISE metrics are in sophistication, particularly for her investment into talent development. Within reputation, Naskar's scores reflect a strong media profile across news and social media suggesting active market status across each practice area. Her work runs roughly 70 to 75 % infrastructure and 30 % private and structured credit, with major recent work for Adani across fundraising, joint ventures, acquisition finance, data centres, and smart meters. The infrastructure mandate has evolved from thermal power and roads into renewables, transmission, and digital infrastructure, with complexity driven by cross-border structuring under India's capital controls and first-of-their-kind financings including a revolving loan structure for smart meters. She works closely with principal associates on client discussions and commercial conversations, maintaining a fluid team structure across multiple partners to give juniors varied experience.

Name: Pranav Sharma
Bio: Placed fifth in the 2026 RISE debt capital market rating, Pranav Sharma's practice stands out for RISE instruction and experience metrics. Across all the RISE metrics, Sharma scores particularly well for a strong client base, social media profile, and interesting approach to pricing. His practice is focused on debt capital markets, covering onshore INR bonds, securitisations, derivatives, and offshore euro bonds, with clients including Kotak, IFC, Blackstone and other leading institutional investors and lenders. His pricing approach involves an internal cost estimation tool applied once key deal parameters are known, part of a broader effort to standardise DCM work by borrowing process discipline from equity teams. Sharma describes his practice as having two defining characteristics: a directness in client communication, including a willingness to deliver difficult messages and a client-first philosophy.

Name: Aashima Johur
Bio:
Aashima Johur at Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas has built her practice around new-age, technology-focused, asset-light companies, working almost exclusively as issuer counsel, a positioning that gives her deeper insight into non-traditional capital structures, employee incentive schemes, and promoter arrangements than a bank-side practice typically produces. Experience and instruction scores are her strongest RISE metric, but she also scores well on some sophistication sub-indicators such as pricing flexibility, and she switches between fixed fees and hourly rates in ways appropriate to client and matter. For pre-IPO advisory work - where the firm entrenches itself with potential issuers up to a year before the formal process begins to assess IPO readiness and identify areas requiring "cleaning up" - the firm tends to work on an hourly basis. 

Johur also scores strongly on the complexity of her mandates in line with her focus on new age companies. Her matters have received a lot of media attention. For example, the Lenskart IPO generated significant social media discussion about valuation and promoter incentives, requiring the team to address public perception and justify the commercial arrangements.

Name: Aayush Mohata
Bio: Aayush Mohata combines strong AI and sophistication scores with a deliberate focus on quality over quantity, building a practice around fewer and larger mandates rather than volume. He uses the firm's KAI platform across a wide range of tasks including preparing diligence checklists, benchmarking disclosures against recent regulatory precedents, and identifying consent triggers in financing documents. Mohata also invests heavily in structured training, running tailored, annual programmes for junior lawyers and bespoke sessions for new joiners. One of his most prominent recent matters is Knowledge Realty Trust's IPO, India's largest REIT and Asia's second largest, sponsored by Blackstone alongside Sattva Developers.

Name: Abhimanyu Bhattacharya
Bio: Abhimanyu Bhattacharya works across the full public markets ecosystem, advising financial sponsors on IPO exits and issuers on listings, with clients including Apollo Global, Blackstone, TPG, and SoftBank. His highest RISE score is for the reputation indicator, where he draws solid peer recommendations, and a visible legal directory presence. His mandates consistently involve sponsor-heavy transactions with large, complex cap tables. Meesho's IPO is a good example: a reverse-flipped issuer with a novel business model where the core challenge was aligning numerous financial sponsors and renegotiating pre-IPO documentation to get the transaction ready for market.

Name: Abhinav Kumar
Bio: In 2024, Abhinav Kumar moved with a senior member team from Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas to build a high quality ECM practice at TT&A. His RISE instruction scores reflect what he has built. Client and deal value scores are particularly strong, showing he has kept a foothold at the upper end of the market with a leaner team. Having watched the deleterious impact of a busy market on lawyers, he keeps a deliberate cap on the number of transactions per associate and invests more heavily in supervision than most of his peers. His standout recent matter is Singtel's sale of shares in Bharti Airtel, acting as the only counsel across two transactions totalling approximately $2.5bn

Name: Abhinav Maker
Bio: Abhinav Maker's instruction and sophistication scores at Trilegal are underpinned by a practice that treats regulatory engagement as a client service rather than a compliance obligation. Maker works directly with SEBI on consultation papers and uses client interviews to shape how his team tracks and communicates regulatory change. AI and data usage is a strong sub-indicator. Maker also prioritises training for his team, which includes mandatory biweekly sessions and knowledge sharing to keep the team current. He has worked on some  novel transactions such as the Bluestone IPO, which required creating new structural mechanisms to meet shareholding rules that a series of funding rounds had made impossible to satisfy in the conventional way, and Dam Capital that became India's first  investment bank to go public. He works in the BFSI, manufacturing, infrastructure, and new-age technology sectors.

Name: Abhiroop Datta
Bio: Abhiroop Datta at Shardul Amarchand Mangaldas & Co scores most strongly on RISE sophistication metrics, with technology usage and talent development the standout sub-indicators. He connects Outlook to Harvey to pull and summarise all correspondence relating to a transaction, replacing manual inbox management, and stores transaction data in structured archives to run analyses without risk. His practice spans large regulated-sector IPOs and technology listings, including matters using SEBI's confidential filing route, with experience across a wide range of promoter profiles and investor types from family-led Indian businesses to international corporate and financial sponsors. He regularly advises on public regulatory compliance issues where multiple regulators or large syndicates are involved. A personal background spanning three countries and ten cities has given him an ability to engage comfortably across socio-economic and cultural contexts, from traditional Indian promoters to sophisticated international clients.

Name: Abhiroop Lahiri
Bio:
Instruction and experience scores are Abhiroop Lahiri's strongest RISE metrics. He joined CAM from IndusLaw in 2024, with a client base that spans small finance banks including Ujjivan and Jana, PE and VC houses exiting through IPOs, and new-age technology companies. 

Lahiri's social media presence is a notable sub-indicator, reflecting a market profile that extends beyond transactions. His practice is deliberately broad across issuer, bank, and selling shareholder mandates, and his healthcare work, including the Doctor Agrawal IPO and Niva Bupa representing Bupa UK's exit, illustrates his range. Everstone Capital is a  particularly valued client, where he acts as promoter counsel across multiple listings in pharma, QSR, and industrial parks.

Name: Aditya George Cheriyan
Bio: Aditya George Cheriyan's is a modern-day practitioner. His highest RISE scores come under sophistication where talent development is his standout sub-indicator. His associate training  covers legal expertise, financial literacy, negotiation, and client communication, Associates are assessed regularly and developed through workshops and partner-led mentoring. Cheriyan teaches externally at Gujarat National Law University, which doubles as a talent pipeline and has contributed to less attrition in his team compared to peers. Clients include Travel Food Services, Sai Life Sciences, and Meesho, each involving novel equity arrangements, regulatory challenges, or cross-border structuring. His team systematically tracks what is market across transactions even where the firm is not instructed.

Name: Albin Thomas
Bio:
Albin Thomas's instruction scores are his strongest RISE metric, built on a practice that spans both ECM and DCM and a track record of first-of-their-kind transactions that generates repeat work.

The Mahindra Finance rights issue, one of the first executed under the new streamlined SEBI regime, and the ROADIS high-yield bond, the first tollway high-yield bond in India, are good illustrations of his work. 

Thomas' AI and data usage is the sophistication standout: his team captures SEBI observations on filed offer documents into a structured database, creating ready-made prompts for drafting teams on what the regulator has been scrutinising in the past six months. 

Clients include Mahindra Group, IIFL Capital, and Jefferies.

Name: Arka Mookerjee
Bio:
Around 90 % of Arka Mookerjee's work at JSA Advocates & Solicitors is independently generated, a figure that is unusual at his level and reflected by his instruction scores in the RISE rating. 

His client roster spans Temasek, for whom he handles all Indian exits, alongside Axis, Kotak, Citi, and JPMorgan. A significant portion of his practice covers government infrastructure mandates. The Raajmarg Infra Investment Trust, the first government-sponsored InvIT in the roads sector, required an entirely new structure to monetise completed road assets outside a standard disinvestment framework, a transaction that illustrates both the complexity and the novelty of his practice.

Name: Bhakta Patnaik
Position: Partner and Head of Capital Markets Practice
Bio: Bhakta Patnaik at Trilegal built the firm's capital markets practice from scratch a decade ago, growing it into a team of close to 100 lawyers that handled 52 listings last year. His instruction scores are his strongest RISE metric, underpinned by a client base that includes issuers, banks and investors such as Blackstone, GIC, ICICI Securities, Bank of America Merrill Lynch, Citigroup, Kedaara Capital and Piramal Group. He is regularly brought in where little precedent exists. Arcil's IPO is a good illustration: the first ever by an asset reconstruction company in India, with a trust-based structure that triggered complex disclosure requirements with no comparable transaction to emulate.

Name: Brajendu Bhaskar
Bio:
Brajendu Bhaskar's client list at Trilegal is his standout sub-indicator among strong instruction and sophistication scores. Client relationships include ICICI Securities, JM Financial, JPMorgan, and Kotak among others. 

The Lenskart IPO is a good illustration of Bhaskar's work standard: a new-age tech company returning market confidence after the post-Paytm lull, with complexity driven by sophisticated investors, a dense cap table, and commercially sensitive negotiations where shareholder rights survive the listing. 

Earlier time at A&O in London gives him useful perspective on international markets that he brings to domestic clients. His team are growing in their AI skills, for example, analysing  international offering documents  to find comparisons for Indian deals where no domestic precedent exists.

Name: Gautham Srinivas
Bio: Gautham Srinivas runs a pure-play ECM practice, covering IPOs, QIPs, and public offerings exclusively, with relationships built around promoters and management teams over the long term. Instruction scores are his strongest RISE metric, with value and client sub-indicators among the highest in this ECM cohort. Srinivas is comfortable working in deals without precedent. For example, Fractal Analytics, which recently listed as one of the world's first AI companies to go public in India, and Aditya Infotech, a $150mn CCTV technology IPO subscribed approximately 100 times. He is committed to his long-term client relationships and not too concerned at only chasing headline transactions at their expense, and keen to build relationships for the practice.

Name: Gokul Rajan
Bio: Gokul Rajan's strongest RISE scores are for experience and instructions, which he performs notably well in both. For his experience score, Rajan received some of the highest scores in the cohort from a top-notch client base - as reflected in his client list. He has worked on some of the largest transactions in the Indian market such as the Hyundai IPO at approximately $3bn, LG India, Zomato, Swiggy and LIC, with Hyundai and LG executed back-to-back. His time with Linklaters, as well as an in-house lawyer at Citi and his M&A work, gives him a big picture approach to managing transactions.

Name: Jabarati Chandra
Bio: India's first insurance company IPO, large-scale QIPs, and tech listings including that of Pine Labs are in Jabarati Chandra's bag. This run of transactions gives her high scores for client base and mandate value in this RISE cohort of ECM lawyers. However, her highest RISE metric comes from her experience scores: her clients really enjoy working with her.  She is strong in the tech sector, and feels it is a sector that requires more than execution prowess and one in which she can exert her fairly unique judgement.  Chandra is deliberate about building a team that mirrors her approach: one that can both digest regulatory updates, provide practical context and then properly advise clients on what actions to take.

Name: Jitesh Shahani
Bio: With extensive experience across IPOs, QIPs, rights offerings and other ECM transactions, Jitesh Shahani at S&R Associates is known for combining a strong technical command of Indian securities regulation with a practical, business-oriented approach. His sophistication scores in the RISE rating are on par with the market. Training and development is the standout sub-indicator alongside a strong client base. His clients value his ability to anticipate regulatory issues and translate evolving frameworks into clear, actionable transaction strategy. Shahani brings substantial international experience to his practice, having worked in Singapore with leading international law firms. His recent representative matters include IPOs and other equity offerings for Afcons Infrastructure, Suzlon Energy, TBO Tek, Pine Labs and Sudeep Pharma.

Name: Juhi Singh
Bio:
The ICICI Prudential AMC IPO, where Juhi Singh represented Prudential as sole selling shareholder in a 100% offer for sale alongside a substantial pre-IPO involving over 28 investors, is the kind of transaction her experience and instruction scores are built on. She also has a high score for the quality of her client base. 

Singh's approach to training her junior lawyers is the sophistication standout: an open-door policy, informal check-ins, and detailed DRHP walkthrough sessions focuses lawyers on understanding substance rather than mechanical drafting. She practitioners who are able to reason through a problem rather than just locating a precedent. 

Her practice is sector-agnostic, but with a recent concentration across tech, manufacturing, healthcare, pharma, insurance, and BFSI.

Name: Lionel D'Almeida
Bio: Lionel D'Almeida has strong market share, as evinced by his instruction scores and a media profile that reflects his role on transactions with public market significance. His client base leans toward private equity and promoter-backed companies, and he advises across the full stakeholder spectrum, acting for issuers, promoters, banks, and PE investors. This breadth allows him nuanced understanding of where transactional tensions are likely to be. Alongside the International Financial Services Authority, he helped shape the regulatory framework for India's GIFT City IFSC. His work at the intersection of practice and policy is useful in a jurisdiction that is still defining its rules.

Name: Madhurima Mukherjee (Saha)
Bio: Madhurima Mukherjee's RISE reputation score reflects a career spent building capital markets practices across several of India's leading firms including Amarchand Mangaldas, Luthra and Luthra and AZB. Now at JSA, she is one of India's most experienced practitioners. Naturally, her RISE Instruction score is high, and she handles both a large volume of work that is high in value.  Recent work with Google and Microsoft is reflective of her quality practice: both engagements required her team to go well beyond standard legal counsel scope, guiding global corporates through the mechanics of the Indian capital markets process in a way that was closer to a banking advisory role than a legal one.

Name: Manan Lahoty
Bio: Manan Lahoty heads India's largest capital markets teams by headcount and receives high numbers of peer recognitions. What distinguishes his practice is a combination of pioneering transaction work and a team that handles demanding mandates without the attrition that afflicts comparable practices. Lahoty led India's first reverse flip IPOs, including PhonePe and Groww, alongside Swiggy, Hella Infra, and Vishal Mega Mart, navigating structural challenges with little precedent. His track record for novel transactions, paired with strong reputation and instruction scores, sets him clearly apart.

Name: Nikhil Naredi
Bio:
Nikhil Naredi at Shardul Amarchand Mangaldas & Co. leads the firm's Mumbai capital markets team alongside Prashant Gupta in Delhi. His reputation scores are his strongest RISE metric. 

Highly regarded by peers, he draws repeat mandates from investment banks and PE houses, including Blackstone and Warburg Pincus portfolio companies. 

Marquee transactions include the Hyundai Motors IPO and the ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund IPO, notable for coordinating one of India's largest syndicates with 18 underwriters. His team holds mandates on two large REIT and InvIT transactions, including a circa $500mn Blackstone portfolio deal currently in progress.

Name: Oishik Bagchi
Bio: Oishik Bagchi scores most strongly on instructions and sophistication in the RISE metrics, with talent development and client base his standout sub-indicators. Bagchi starts his junior training programme each June with new hires, running frequent sessions on drafting and capital markets processes, supplemented by a course in partnership with Gujarat National Law University. It blends online and offline teaching and functions as a recruitment pipeline. His client base spans, amongst others, Morgan Stanley, Citi, Goldman Sachs, and ICICI Securities on the banking side and Vikram Solar, FirstCry (Brainbees Solutions Limited), Tenneco and Smartworks on the company side, with clients often retained post-IPO for follow-on work. The Vikram Solar IPO, executed in a nascent renewable energy sector with limited precedent and disputes managed in court late in the timeline, illustrates the complexity of his practice.

Name: Pracheta Bhattacharya
Bio:
Pracheta Bhattacharya's instruction scores are his strongest RISE metric at JSA, with client base and deal value among the strongest sub-indicators, built on relationships with IIFL, Axis, Kotak, and other domestic and international merchant banks. 

Bhattacharya's practice spans equity capital markets with InvITs and REITs as a core specialism, an unusual combination that few practitioners in this cohort have developed to the same depth. 

What distinguishes his current client profile is a deliberate concentration on government-linked infrastructure mandates, including the Ministry of Coal and Ministry of Roads, at a moment when disinvestment targets are becoming more aggressive. The NHAI fundraise at approximately $560mn illustrates that positioning.

Name: Prashant Rajput
Bio: Prashaant Rajput at Economic Laws Practice achieves his strongest RISE scores in the sophistication of his practice. He uses AI to scan and compare transactional documents across repeat mandates, alongside interactive training that grounds juniors in the real impact of regulatory changes on live transactions. The Waterways Leisure IPO, a luxury cruise listing with no Indian market comparator, illustrates his practice well: a niche sector requiring careful pre-emptive drafting and disclosure work. Rajput is candid about managing egos across a transactional team and views collaborative problem-solving, including owning mistakes, as central to getting deals done.

Name: Prashant Gupta
Position: National Practice Head of Capital Markets
Bio:
Prashant Gupta  is the number one ranked equity capital markets RISE lawyer in this year's cohort. He received more peer recommendations than any other practitioner. The majority (80%) of his book of work is on deals above $250mn, and he acts as issuer counsel on large marquee technology and internet transactions, as well as for global multinationals. 

The complexity on these technology and internet mandates tends to come from regulator scepticism toward loss-making companies, fragmented cap tables, and shareholders who all want different things. 

His issuer counsel role on one of India's largest IPOs for  Hyundai at approximately $3bn illustrates the level at which he operates.

Name: Priyadarshini Rao
Bio:
Priyadarshini Rao's client score is among the highest in this cohort at Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas. Her base includes ICICI Prudential AMC, ICICI Bank, Blackstone, JPMorgan, Kotak, Nomura, and UBS across equity capital markets and a recently expanded InvIT practice. 

Instruction scores are Rao's strongest RISE metric, although she also scores well in the sophistication indicators. Her approach to pricing approach stands out. Pricing benchmarks are set at the partnership level but adjusted for client importance and strategic value.

One of the younger practitioners in this cohort, Rao's willingness to move into new sectors before they become crowded is what her client and deal flow numbers appear to suggest.

Name: Ravi Dubey
Bio: Ravi Dubey's practice is product-driven rather than client-driven, built across IPOs, QIPs, rights issues, and block trades for a both investment banks and issuers. Instruction scores are his strongest RISE metric, with sub-indicators showing high deal value and notable volume for a practitioner who is deliberate about mandate selection. His client base spans large Indian corporates, family-owned promoter groups, and PE-backed companies preparing to list, often with intricate promoter structures and cross-border elements. His sweet spot is anticipating regulations. He reads the intent behind rules rather than their surface application, helping clients get ahead of problems.

Name: Reuben Chacko
Bio:
Based in Bangalore, Reuben Chacko co-leads CAM's South India capital markets team. Chacko is a very forward thinking lawyer and heavy AI user. He is also actively involved in recruitment and now assesses law students for their AI familiarity and proficiency. Other external roles include doing guest lectures in law schools (capital markets / securities law) a few times per year.

In the RISE rating, his highest scores are in the instructions and sophistication set of indicators. His standout sub indicator is his usage of AI and data, where he is a keen experimenter. One of his use cases was to use AI to identify lender consent requirements (change of shareholding/management/control) across hundreds of bulky financing agreements (common for NBFC/MFI issuers). He predicts it has saved ~60–70% of time versus manual review.

Chacko also scores very strongly on work complexity: he works in highly regulated sectors such as pharma and healthcare where IPOs/QIPs are challenging, and due diligence involves extensive operational approvals and compliance review. Recent deals include Anthem Biosciences IPO and Biocon Limited's QIP.

Name: Richa Choudhary
Bio: Richa Choudhary at Trilegal has near-perfect instruction scores, with a client base score that reflects their blue chip quality.  She works across both equity and debt capital markets and scores highest overall for volume of matters while maintaining strong deal quality. One of her most distinctive transactions is the first listing on India's Social Stock Exchange, advising SGBS Unnati Foundation on a Zero Coupon Zero Principal instrument IPO without a guiding playbook. The structure attracted enough international attention that the Malaysian Stock Exchange approached her team for guidance on setting up a comparable framework.

Name: Sandip Bhagat
Bio:
Sandip Bhagat is one of the most experienced practitioners in Indian capital markets, with his name above the door and a three-decade career spanning US securities law, Indian transaction structuring, and active involvement in SEBI's Primary Markets Committee. 

His instruction scores are his strongest RISE metric and he scores highest for client base and deal value, built on long-standing relationships with major domestic and international investment banks including ICICI, Axis, Citi, Goldman Sachs, and Morgan Stanley. 

Notable recent work includes advising Prudential as sole selling shareholder in the ICICI Prudential AMC IPO, a transaction with meaningful cross-border complexity given Prudential's UK and Hong Kong listings.

Name: Sudhir Bassi
Bio: Membership of SEBI's Primary Market Advisory Committee gives Sudhir Bassi a direct line into regulatory policy. His work on ESOP grandfathering rules for founders reclassified as promoters is a concrete example of that influence translating into outcomes for clients. Instruction and experience scores are his strongest RISE metrics, with client base and complexity the standout sub-indicators. His practice focuses on multinational subsidiaries seeking Indian listings and mature PE-backed companies, a client profile that tends to generate structurally demanding work. Blackstone is a named repeat client, which gives his team a rounded experience and Bassi has a steady pipeline of deals in the $300mn to $800mn range.

Name: Thomas George
Bio: George's client list says it all. In the Pine Labs IPO, he advised approximately 17 selling shareholders including Peak XV, Mastercard, and PayPal, and his banking relationships span JM Financial, Kotak, ICICI Securities, Citi, Morgan Stanley, JPMorgan, and Goldman Sachs. His RISE strengths are in his instruction scores, where his client base is a standout sub-indicator and in his sophistication scores. His work on the WeWork India and Indicube listings, reveal a sub-specialism in a pocket of the market that has genuine structural complexity. In terms of the sophistication of his practice, his team uses AI for consent checks across financing contracts, diligence verification, and first-pass document review, allowing senior lawyers to focus on higher-value legal work.

Name: Varoon Chandra
Bio: Varoon Chandra leads AZB's capital markets practice and scores most strongly on instructions and experience, with volume and value sub-indicators that reflect a consistent flow of complex mandates rather than high-profile work. His focus is primarily ECM, with DCM taken on selectively where the complexity warrants it. His client base spans Tata Capital, Hexaware, Swiggy, Canara Robeco, and Infosys across a sector-agnostic practice that includes financial services, technology, consumer, and asset management.

Name: Veena Sivaramakrishnan
Bio: Veena Sivaramakrishnan's practice stands especially strong in instructions, completing work for a great set of clients. Recent completed transactions include India's largest debt raise, at approximately $3bn, for Shapoorji Pallonji Group as well as one the country's largest FDI mandates in Indian financial services by Emirates Bank. As seen across the RISE sophistication metrics, Sivarakmakrishnan also takes an interesting approach to pricing: most work is billed on a fixed-fee basis, with 15 to 20 % of matters charged hourly during early-stage structuring, and pricing data tracked through finance systems to inform future fee predictions. She notes that she is deliberate about knowing when to intervene in a transaction and when to let her team take ownership, staying current on regulatory developments as the foundation for both.

Name: Vijay Parthasarathi
Bio:
Vijay Parthasarathi  leads a 25-lawyer practice and his instruction scores are his strongest metric, with consistently high-value mandates. 

His practice covers technology, fintech, consumer and retail, real estate, renewables, and NBFCs. Both the recent PhonePe IPO, where his team represented PhonePe in September 2025, and the Brookfield REIT QIP, one of the largest real estate QIPs completed in India in December 2025, are standout recent matters, alongside a Piramal Group reverse merger listing that required navigating listing mechanics without a traditional IPO process.

Name: Vinay Sirohia
Bio: Vinay Sirohia has a good track record, able to handle complex, drawn-out transactions such as the Indo-MIM IPO.  His background spans equity, debt, and overseas financing programmes, and earlier transactions include the Gland Pharma IPO, the first Chinese-driven IPO in India, and Bharti Airtel's $9bn acquisition debt refinancing. Besides his strong market share, he is also a keen mentor. Junior lawyers are brought to pitch meetings and encouraged to build relationships with bankers early. His practice is sector-agnostic, covering tech, fintech, telecom, defence, manufacturing, staffing, pharma, healthcare, and retail.

Name: Vishal Yaduvanshi
Bio:
Vishal Yaduvanshi's  instruction scores are his strongest RISE metric. and he scores particularly highly for the quality of his client base, with a consistent track record of high-value mandates. His work includes PhonePe, the largest Indian payments gateway company at a $1.5bn IPO and $10 to $15bn valuation, and Physics Wallah, the first education technology company to go public in India at approximately $500mn, a listing that he says has materially restored market confidence in the edtech sector. 

Yaduvanshi is passionate about building cohesive teams. He runs quarterly social evenings and monthly meetings focused on the bigger picture rather than transactions, an approach he sees as central to retaining good people in a competitive market.

Name: Vishnu Dutt U
Bio:
Dutt has an integrated securities law practice, and deep understanding of how SEBI and SAT will rule on certain matters, enhancing the quality of his diligence and disclosures for clients. His highest RISE metric is in instructions. Key clients include mid-sized investment banks such as Ecmoris, MK Capital, IIFL, Centrum, Dam Capital, and Motilal Oswal, with Motilal Oswal and IIFL gaining a lion's share of medium-sized deals (in the 500-1,000 crore range) over the last 18 months.

Dutt's other high-scoring sub indicator was in the development of junior lawyers. Training extends beyond capital markets, reflecting the firm's philosophy of "holistic lawyering". For example, capital markets lawyers review banking laws and attend sessions on labour codes and the new Securities Market Code. Dutt emphasises that capital markets is a practical discipline with a steep learning curve in the first 12-18 months. The firm consciously ensures that juniors understand the rationale behind templates rather than simply relying on precedent-driven drafting.

Significant recent matters included a CNC manufacturer IPO notable for its high-profile client list including Apple, Ferrari, Rolls Royce and defence manufacturers.

Name: Yash J Ashar
Bio: Yash J Ashar has the highest number of peer recommendations and carries one of the strongest combined reputation and instruction scores in the Indian capital markets category. His practice spans IPOs, QIPs, and securities regulation. His ability to navigate SEBI and stock exchange scrutiny is commended by his peers. Ashar personally leads high-value transactions for clients such as Tata Capital, Swiggy, and Hyundai India, remaining closely involved through disclosure, regulatory, and structural complexity.

Name: Pratichi Mishra
Bio:
Sitting on SEBI standing committees for business trust regulation is unusual for a partner at Pratichi Mishra's career stage, and that proactive and direct regulatory engagement is what her sophistication scores and the quality of her infrastructure advisory work appear to reflect. 

Instructions are Mishra's strongest RISE metric. High scores for client base and deal value are built on some of India's largest infrastructure transactions including a road-sector investment of approximately $1.1bn and multiple fundraises exceeding $500mn. Her practice spans the full lifecycle of infrastructure and real estate platforms rather than a single stage of capital markets work, which is itself a differentiator.

Mishra also scores highly in terms of the way she runs her practice. With her associates, she involves them in conferences and policy related work, including regulatory research and drafting. They are required to write monitor and analyse regulatory updates within 24 to 48 hours of changes, a training philosophy that mirrors her own relationship with the regulatory process.

Name: Abhay Joshi
Bio:
Abhay Joshi's strongest RISE metric is instructions, driven by the exceptional complexity of mandates he handles for a high-quality client roster. His portfolio includes IREL India, Bridgestone India, SKF India, Ecozen Solutions, Corteva Agriscience, and United Breweries. His practice is defined by these layered enforcement matters rather than routine market-share analysis, reflecting a deep institutional understanding of Indian competition law.

Joshi differentiates himself through his "quiet achiever" status; while his reputation score is more minimal, his work on sophisticated regulatory intersections is notable. He recently defended IREL in a monopoly abuse case, arguing that strategic policy context justifies market position. His work for United Breweries involved navigating a highly regulated industry to address complex business issues regarding the sharing of commercially sensitive pricing and production data. Having transitioned from a background in M&A and private equity, Joshi also leverages a growing strand of data privacy work. He believes this forward-facing approach, treating privacy as a non-price parameter in competition analysis, is essential for modern market power mandates.

Name: Abir Roy
Bio:
Abir Roy at Sarvada Legal occupies an unusual position in the Indian competition market: an enforcement-led practice running approximately 80% contentious work, with a distinct concentration on the complainant side in big tech platform cases at a time when most comparable complexity sits within larger full-service firms.

Abir's work on Google, Apple and app ecosystem matters involves technical arguments around platform economics, data flows and jurisdictional overlap with data protection, with data accumulation treated as a source of dominance rather than a separate regulatory question. A notable feature is his representation of app developer communities acting collectively against dominant platforms, requiring coordination of multi-party complainant strategies across long-running, multi-forum disputes. He argues his own matters and maintains full file ownership throughout, a model that suits the litigation-control and forum-strategy dimension of enforcement work but operates from a smaller platform than the full-service practices handling comparable complexity.

Name: Akshat Kulshrestha
Bio: Akshat Kulshrestha at S&R Associates scores most strongly on RISE instruction metrics, driven by technically demanding merger control work and growing enforcement exposure rather than filing volume alone. His Client base is the standout sub-indicator, spanning infrastructure, private equity, and logistics, alongside cartel defence and High Court litigation for a top global express courier company. RISE sophistication scores are the second strongest signal. AI usage is notable, with the team building a competition-specific repository of CCI precedents segmented by conduct type, penalty range, and market definition using Lucio.

Name: Anisha Chand
Bio:
Placed 15th in the 2026 RISE competition list, Anisha Chand has high sophistication and experience scores. Within the sophistication score, Chand's attention to talent development and approach to pricing reflect a practice with little attrition rate and encouragement to junior lawyers to experiment AI within upskilling courses. Chand's instruction scores with client quality and mandate value as the leading contributing metrics. Her instructions profile points to consistent flow from large strategic and sponsor-led transactions, with the team among the most active in Indian merger control by filing volume and particularly prominent in long-form notifications.

The practice is notable for sitting where scale and complexity meet: form-two filings, borderline-modification matters and transactions where the work centres on data-heavy advocacy to avoid remedies rather than reacting to a clearance problem already in motion. Beyond merger control, Chand maintains a full-spectrum competition practice spanning cartel matters, leniency work, complainant-side mandates and compliance programmes for global platforms, with a developing strand of advisory at the competition-privacy intersection.

Name: Anshuman Sakle
Bio:
Anshuman Sakle’s reputation is defined by exceptional visibility; he is one of the most frequently quoted practitioners in the legal press and maintains an excellent profile across international legal directories and peer mentions. His instructions score is equally robust, reflecting a high-volume, high-complexity practice that handled 17 major transactions in 2025 for a prestigious client roster including Torrent Pharmaceuticals, Mahindra & Mahindra, Haldiram Snack Foods, Interpublic Group (IPG), Wilmar International, and American Axle & Manufacturing.

Based in Mumbai, Sakle’s practice typically splits 75% toward merger control and 25% toward enforcement, though he notes this ratio fluctuates with the Commission’s investigative cycles. He differentiates himself through a culture of team continuity; by giving associates early client-facing responsibility, he maintains notably low attrition rates. This stability, combined with his long-standing engagement with the CCI, provides clients with reliable regulatory navigation across diverse sectors such as pharma, financial services, and oil and gas.

Name: Aparna Mehra
Bio:
Aparna Mehra ranks within the top 20 of the RISE competition list, with her strongest metric being reputation. This standing is driven by consistent recognition across legal directories and a notable social media presence, alongside a solid profile among her peers. Her instructions score is also robust; while she maintains an understated profile, she remains a trusted advisor for high-caliber clients including ArcelorMittal, Bain Capital Investors, Jindal Steel & Power, Rolls-Royce India, and Vedanta Limited.

As one of the first generation of Indian competition specialists with two decades of experience, Mehra's practice has a distinct cross-border character. She is frequently sought out as local counsel by firms in Japan, Brussels, London, and New York for specialist mandates. She believes her practice is valued for its reliability and deep system-level understanding of Indian merger control. This expertise is evident in her analysis of deal value thresholds and evolving green channel filing behaviors, reflecting a sophisticated grasp of the regulatory landscape that transcends individual mandates.

Name: Avaantika Kakkar
Bio: Avaantika Kakkar's strongest RISE metrics are reputation and instructions, with high scores driven by consistent peer recognition, strong directory presence and sustained demand from sophisticated clients. Her practice spans the Commission, appellate forums, High Courts and the Supreme Court, a breadth of forum coverage within a single practice that differentiates her in a market where many competition partners operate more narrowly. She is focused on complex, data-heavy merger control work across healthcare, infrastructure and industrial sectors, where market definition and competitive effects are actively contested. She operates a lean staffing model, giving associates and partners greater ownership of matters. On the enforcement side, she has been involved in a number of the regulator's most significant dawn raids, including instructions from clients outside the firm's existing base, reflecting trust in high-pressure situations.

Name: Avinash Amarnath
Bio: Avinash Amarnath's scores highest in instructions, and is particularly strong for its complexity, followed by client quality and volume metrics. Amarnath's practice has a greater emphasis on complex enforcement work and litigation mandates, for a client mix that includes Google, Intel and major participants in the Amazon and Flipkart investigations, alongside significant corporate groups and media distribution clients. Around half the practice is international, with working relationships spanning Allen Overy Shearman, Freshfields, Linklaters, Davis Polk, and Stibbe. His work sits at the intersection of competition law, administrative law and sector regulation, with a particular concentration in digital markets, telecom-media disputes, leniency and cartel defence.Notable matters include acting on what is understood to be India's first commitments application under the new settlements framework, the Asianet Digital jurisdictional dispute requiring analysis of competing regulatory regimes at the Supreme Court level, and the seller-side challenge in the Amazon and Flipkart investigations raising fundamental questions of CCI procedure and due process.

Name: Bharat Budholia
Bio:
Bharat Budholia ranks 10th in the RISE competition rating, with instructions serving as his strongest metric. This standing is driven by a prestigious client roster and a near-equal balance between high volume and technical complexity. Based in Mumbai, Budholia is structurally positioned at the center of the city's private equity deal flow, representing heavyweights such as KKR and TPG, alongside Mankind Pharma, Citibank, Siemens, Qube Cinema, the BCCI, and the IATA.

His reputation is exceptionally high, supported by strong peer recommendations and a consistent profile across legal directories and the legal press. Approximately 60% of his work focuses on merger control, where he differentiates himself by rejecting template-driven filings in favour of bespoke, sector-specific narratives. Within the sophistication category, his use of technology is a practical marker of quality; he has developed searchable databases of past advisory case law and sector-specific notes to ensure institutional consistency.

Name: Deeksha Manchanda
Bio: Deeksha Manchanda at Chandhiok & Mahajan scores most strongly on RISE instruction and sophistication metrics, with volume and technology the standout indicators. This is an unusual pairing that points to a practitioner embedded in technically demanding, long-running enforcement matters rather than one whose profile is driven by market pull or transaction flow. Her practice runs 60/40 in favour of behavioural, advisory, and enforcement work over merger control. Google is the clearest centrepiece, with multiple overlapping matters since 2020, a coordinated appeals strategy, and a substantial penalty reduction among the outcomes. JCB, Michelin, and Coca-Cola feature alongside, several on matters stretching across years rather than single mandates. That sticking power on complex, regulator-facing disputes is the most distinctive feature of the practice. She integrates AI tools actively into her workflow and takes a hands-on approach to team development and internal culture-building.

Name: Dhruv Rajain
Bio: Dhruv Rajain at Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas scores most strongly on RISE instructions metrics, a profile that fits a hands-on merger control practitioner whose value lies in skilled regulatory navigation and technical filing work. His deal exposure spans healthcare, financial services, data centres, paper and pulp, and infrastructure. Notable matters include a data centre transaction, understood to be the first CCI review of that market with conditions imposed, and an ITC-related paper and pulp filing requiring close market analysis in a concentrated sector. A roads and infrastructure matter in which the team persuaded the CCI to extend post-facto treatment to a unit acquisition structure in a novel way adds a first-instance dimension to his instruction profile. His RISE sophistication metrics are also strong, particularly in pricing, where he adopts a flexible approach combining hourly and fixed-fee models depending on the mandate. He places particular weight on understanding the combinations division's internal processes and maintaining working relationships with the review team.

Name: Ela Bali
Bio:
Ela Bali's achieves an exceptionally high RISE instructions score that distinguishes her within the cohort. This standing is driven by a prestigious client roster and her involvement in highly complex, contentious mandates. She represents global investors and corporations, including Temasek, EQT, Hyundai, Ford Motor Company, Alpha Wave Global, and IHC Abu Dhabi. A standout example of her high-value work is the Temasek and Alpha Wave investment in Haldiram's, one of the largest transactions in India's FMCG sector.

Her practice is split evenly between merger control and enforcement, with notable instructions including the MakeMyTrip-Oyo MFN matter and the Hyundai spare parts aftermarket litigation. Bali's sophistication score is her second-strongest metric, reflecting her expert use of AI tools for research and document summarization alongside JSA's internal knowledge management systems. While she remains a high achiever in terms of mandate complexity and volume, her reputation score reflects a distinctly more moderate public presencm with only some profile across legal directories.

Name: Gaurav Desai
Bio:
Gaurav Desai's highest RISE metrics are in experience, driven by a practice where clients value his grounded, operational approach to mandates. His instructions score is equally robust, reflecting a portfolio of work for sovereign wealth funds, leading automotive manufacturers, and major financial institutions, including Fairfax and HDFC Capital Advisors. He also regularly advises global conglomerates such as Johnson & Johnson.

Desai splits his practice between corporate M&A and competition law, with a primary focus on merger control. He differentiates himself through his practical analysis of India's deal value thresholds; he notes that while these were designed for digital and asset-light transactions, they are increasingly capturing traditional brick-and-mortar sectors in ways the CCI did not fully anticipate.
